label: Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models

node.label: Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models
node.definition: Energy-based, Hopfield, and Boltzmann models assign scalar compatibility to configurations and perform recall or generation by moving toward lower-energy states.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models is to connect Energy-based objectives, Recurrence & feedback state to downstream capabilities including Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection.
examples: substrate for Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Energy-based objectives, Recurrence & feedback state.

dependencies.rationale: Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Energy-based objectives.
Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Recurrence & feedback state.
Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Differentiable memory read/write.
